behaviors.description: The primary translator produces a grammatically coherent translation that preserves the meaning of the source text, not just a word-for-word substitution.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Agentic, because correctness depends on real neural translation output quality (grammar + meaning preservation), which a stub cannot validate. MOCK-HIDE: mocked translator outputs would always pass quality checks if fixtures are curated. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration) rejected: behavior text explicitly tests linguistic quality, not routing/retries/payload plumbing. Counter-argument (up to Workflow) rejected: this is a single model judgment, not multiple agentic steps chained.
behaviors.test_description: Run a curated multilingual evaluation set through the real translation model/API and score adequacy/fluency against reference translations (or blinded human rubric). Fail if coherence/meaning thresholds are not met.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A lower-level test with stubs cannot expose real model-quality regressions this behavior targets.

behaviors.description: When the translation API returns a 503 error, the service transparently falls back to the dictionary-based translator without returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: System, because failure boundary includes real HTTP 503 handling from external API path plus fallback behavior. REAL-INFRA default applies: real HTTP status/error mapping can differ from mocked exceptions. MOCK-HIDE: a fake client that directly throws a known error can hide transport-layer/status-parsing bugs. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Integration) rejected: behavior text names concrete HTTP 503. Counter-argument (up to Agentic) rejected: this behavior checks error-handling orchestration, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Start service with local SQLite dictionary and point primary translator client to a test HTTP endpoint that returns 503. Send request; assert 200 response with dictionary translation and no caller-visible error, with fallback path telemetry recorded.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Lower-level mocked-client tests can miss real HTTP-to-domain error translation failures.

behaviors.description: The dictionary-based fallback returns the correct pre-stored translation for phrases that exist in the SQLite database.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because the behavior is module handoff between fallback translator and real SQLite persistence/query layer. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess beyond local DB wiring; (b) no AI output dependence; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM stage handoff. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Unit) rejected: correctness depends on real schema/query/encoding interaction with SQLite, not pure function logic. Counter-argument (up to System) rejected: no external HTTP/subprocess requirement in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed a test SQLite DB with known phrase mappings, invoke fallback translator through service/repository wiring, and assert exact returned translation for each seeded phrase.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit tests of lookup logic alone would miss DB schema/query mismatches.

behaviors.description: The quality tracking comparison correctly computes edit distance between primary and fallback outputs and stores the metric.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because behavior spans algorithm + persistence (compute metric then store). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real network/subprocess needed; (b) no dependence on AI quality if fixed strings are supplied; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM-orchestration dependency. TWO-PASS counter-argument (down to Unit) rejected: storing metric requires real repository boundary. Counter-argument (up to Agentic/System) rejected: behavior text is deterministic computation and storage, not model judgment or real external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixed primary/fallback strings with known expected character edit distance; execute quality tracker end-to-end with test DB; assert persisted metric equals expected value and is linked to the translation event.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: A pure unit test of edit-distance math would not catch persistence wiring/serialization failures.

behaviors.description: When both translators are available, the service returns the primary translation and logs the quality comparison in the background.
behaviors.justification: PASS1: Integration, because this is orchestration/state handoff across local modules (primary result selection + async quality logging). INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) behavior can be validated without real external network by using deterministic translator adapters; (b) assertion does not depend on model output quality, only source selection and background side effect; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM decision governing handoff correctness. TWO-PASS counter-argument (up to System) rejected: behavior text does not require real HTTP semantics, only availability and orchestration. Counter-argument (down to Unit) rejected: async background logging plus persistence crosses module boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service with deterministic primary/fallback adapters and real test DB/queue. Invoke translation, assert response uses primary output immediately, then assert background job persisted quality-comparison log for the same request.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Unit tests cannot reliably catch async orchestration and persistence handoff defects.
